subject: Gaming
title: The Famicom: Japan's Secret Weapon
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Famicom: Japan's Secret Weapon.

hook.text: The Famicom launched in Japan in 1983—the same year gaming died in America. Timing is everything.

buildup.text: Nintendo's Family Computer was smaller, cheaper, and more colorful than any American console.

discovery.text: Japanese developers created games with depth and polish. Super Mario Bros. was the proof of concept.

twist.text: While America declared gaming dead, the Famicom sold 19 million units in Japan. Two different realities.

climax.text: The Famicom's success gave Nintendo confidence to invade America—and save the industry.

punchline.text: Gaming died in America. In Japan, it was thriving. Perspective matters.

quiz.question: What was happening in Japan while gaming crashed in America?
options: The Famicom was selling millions and gaming was thriving
Japan had no gaming market yet
